title: **Subject: California Public Records Act Request (Follow-up Request)**

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: **Subject: California Public Records Act Request (Follow-up Request)**
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

**Subject: California Public Records Act Request (Follow-up Request)**

To Whom It May Concern,

This is a follow-up request under the California Public Records Act.

This request specifically pertains to the CAD report (Incident No. 1310300036) that was disclosed to me on March 24, 2026, via email from Robert M. Myers (sent from [myers_robert@smc.edu](mailto:swheed@gmail.com)).

I respectfully request copies of the following records, whether in electronic or physical form:

1. All written statements authored by or attributed to me (Jung, Young Jin), including any drafts or revisions, and including those referenced in the above-mentioned CAD report as “written statements between SMC student Young-Jin ‘Peter’ Jung and the Human Resources department personnel.”

2. All email communications referencing me, including but not limited to communications between Santa Monica College personnel, Human Resources, campus police, and any third parties. This specifically includes the “copies of E-mails that are of concern” referenced in the CAD report, as well as all email communications exchanged between SMC Human Resources personnel and Jung, Young Jin (“Peter” Jung), without limitation.

3. Any and all notes, reports, narratives, memoranda, or contemporaneous records prepared by responding officers or personnel regarding statements attributed to me, including any notes documenting statements I made to law enforcement at the time of the police response (i.e., officer notes taken during or immediately after the interaction), including those associated with the above-referenced CAD report.

In addition, I request:

4. All internal communications, including emails, messages, or correspondence among staff, administrators, HR, campus police, Crisis Team, and Judicial Affairs relating to me.

5. All draft versions, revisions, and redlined copies of any reports or documents concerning me.

6. All metadata associated with the requested records, including but not limited to creation date, modification history, authorship, and file properties.

7. The original complaints, allegations, and any witness statements made against me, including the exact wording of such complaints.

8. Any records reflecting the application of institutional policies or procedures to my case, including notices, determinations, and disciplinary-related documentation.

9. Any audio recordings, photographs, surveillance footage, or other media evidence relating to the incident(s) in question.

If any portion of these records is withheld or redacted, please provide:

* The specific legal basis for each withholding or redaction, and
* All reasonably segregable portions of otherwise exempt records.

If these records have been previously provided, please identify when and how they were provided and confirm whether the production was complete.

I request that all records be provided in electronic format where available.

Thank you for your time and compliance with this request.

Sincerely,
Jung, Young Jin

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Young Jin Jung
